vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data confirms a critical failure in the biome registry. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. The pots appear to contain only soil, debris, or are entirely obscured by darkness/lack of biomass. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor; the plant itself is not discernible.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no Pothos foliage detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, potentially saturated or high-organic content; no visible moisture gradients.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white particulate matter (possibly perlite or eggshell fragments) on the surface of the lower pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the earliest image suggests human intervention/maintenance, but no plant growth is supported by the current visual evidence.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero evidence of vegetative growth, recovery, or movement. The biome remains in a state of total stasis or decay.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens are either dead, dormant, or missing. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue indicates a total collapse of the indoor garden system.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete absence of the expected botanical specimens despite the presence of the pots and the scale anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the indoor botanical collection. From the earliest image to the current state, the pots remain devoid of any identifiable plant life. The white rabbit anchor in the black pot is the only consistent element, yet it sits in a barren environment. No signs of germination or recovery are present.
